- **Pre-planned ex parte filing** (Albert Watson's police report admission) is a smoking gun.
## MOTION TO SET ASIDE EX PARTE ORDERS (MCR 2.612(C)(1))
- **Statistical evidence** (44% ex parte rate, all against Father) is powerful pattern evidence.
- **Court may distinguish** between different ex parte orders, granting relief on some but not others.
### Response 5: "The Ex Parte Orders Were Necessary for Child Safety"
| 44% ex parte order rate | **Father** | ★★★★ |
| Rusco ex parte communication with HealthWest | **Father** | ★★★★ |
- Issue of first impression: Systematic ex parte order abuse in family court
| Motion to Set Aside Ex Parte | All ex parte orders vacated | Denied; partial vacatur | 20–30% at trial; 50%+ on appeal | Low — strongest appellate issue | **MEDIUM-HIGH** |
3. **Vacatur of August 8, 2025 ex parte orders** — or stipulation that they are superseded
2. **Vacatur of ALL ex parte orders**
- Exposure of systemic ex parte abuse patterns
3. **Frame as family court reform** — "44% ex parte order rate is not justice"

constitutional_violations.description: Parenting time suspended via ex parte order without notice or hearing — 52 ex parte orders total with 44% rate, 100% favoring Emily Watson
constitutional_violations.actors: Judge McNeill; Emily Watson; Jennifer Barnes
constitutional_violations.evidence: Ex parte orders; court docket; statistical analysis
constitutional_violations.caselaw: Mathews v. Eldridge, 424 U.S. 319 (1976) — three-factor balancing test for procedural due process; Fuentes v. Shevin, 407 U.S. 67 (1972) — prior notice and hearing required before deprivation
constitutional_violations.michigan_auth: MCR 3.207(A) — requirements for ex parte orders in domestic relations; In re Rood, 483 Mich 73 (2009) — due process in family proceedings

constitutional_violations.description: Ex parte order based on recording made in violation of MCL 750.539c (felony eavesdropping) by Lori Watson — fruit of the poisonous tree
constitutional_violations.actors: Lori Watson; Judge McNeill; Emily Watson
constitutional_violations.evidence: Illegal recording; MCL 750.539c; ex parte motion
constitutional_violations.caselaw: Mapp v. Ohio, 367 U.S. 643 (1961) — exclusionary rule; Wong Sun v. United States, 371 U.S. 471 (1963) — fruit of the poisonous tree
constitutional_violations.michigan_auth: People v. Beavers, 393 Mich 554 (1975) — Michigan eavesdropping; MCL 750.539c — felony, up to 2 years

constitutional_violations.description: 52 ex parte orders with 100% granted to mother (Emily) and 0% to father (Andrew) — statistical impossibility absent discrimination. 44% ex parte rate far exceeds Michigan average.
constitutional_violations.evidence: Court docket analysis; statistical comparison to state averages
constitutional_violations.caselaw: Craig v. Boren, 429 U.S. 190 (1976) — intermediate scrutiny for gender; United States v. Virginia, 518 U.S. 515 (1996) — exceedingly persuasive justification required
constitutional_violations.michigan_auth: MCL 722.23 — best interest factors make no gender distinction; MCR 2.003 — disqualification for bias
